Form.name: Form_VSNT_Create_New_Test_Account
Form.Description: This form is used to create new test account

Field.helpKey: Start Date cannot be a past date
Field.name: testAccountStartDate

Field.helpKey: Effective End Date can be at most 2 months from the Start Date
Field.name: testAccountEndDate

Field.displayName: Do you need an Active Directory Account
Field.name: ADAccountOption

Script.Source: return "false";

Field.name: approvalSchemeField
Field.Description: This field is dynamic based on the type of workflow, as the approval scheme values is the only real difference between what we need for Create and Update vs. normal provisioning. The type is injected into the rule/script context by the WorkflowBean.

Field.name: approvalScheme
Field.Description: This is the value that gets set on the arg value.

entry.value: script: return approvalSchemeField == null || sailpoint.tools.Util.nullSafeContains( approvalSchemeField, "none");

Script.Source: return value;
import sailpoint.workflow.FormUtil; return FormUtil.getStepElectronicSignatureNames(context, field, stepDTO, locale);
import sailpoint.workflow.FormUtil; return FormUtil.getStepElectronicSignatureNames(context, field, stepDTO, locale);
import sailpoint.workflow.FormUtil; return FormUtil.getStepElectronicSignatureNames(context, field, stepDTO, locale);
import sailpoint.workflow.FormUtil; return FormUtil.getStepElectronicSignatureNames(context, field, stepDTO, locale);

Field.name: workItemHoursBetweenReminders
Field.Description:
This is the value required by the Workflow engine, using the field above convert the days into hours.
Workflow.ARG_WORK_ITEM_HOURS_BETWEEN_REMINDERS

Field.name: workItemHoursTillEscalation
Field.Description:
This is required for both escallation AND reminders, don't let the attribute name fool you.
The way it works is that the WorkItem has to sit for hoursTillEscalation before the process begins.
Then a "clock" starts and on each tick we first send reminders up to maxReminders, then we run the escalation rule to change owners. After the owner change we send reminders again on each tick up to maxReminders and then run the escalation rule again.
If the approvalEscalation == sendReminders OR reminderEsclate we use the workItemDaysBeforeReminderField field If the approvalEscalation == escalateOnly we use the workItemDaysTillEscalationField field
We ignore negative number conversion because that represent seconds.

Form.name: Application Account by Attribute Report Form
Role Profiles Details Report Custom Fields
Roles by Application Report Custom Fields

Script.Source: return !sailpoint.tools.Util.isEmpty(applications);
return sailpoint.tools.Util.isEmpty(applications) && (showAllApps == null || !showAllApps);

Form.name: Roles by Entitlement Report Custom Fields

Script.Source: return (profileType == null || profileType.equals("Permission"));
return (profileType == null || profileType.equals("Permission"));
return (profileType == null || profileType.equals("Entitlement"));
return (profileType == null || profileType.equals("Entitlement"));
return (profileType == null);
import sailpoint.object.*; return WorkItem.State.getWorkItemStatesList();

Form.name: Application Owner Access Review Report Form
Role Membership Access Review Report Form
Role Composition Access Review Report Form
Account Group Membership Access Review Report Form
Account Group Permissions Access Review Report Form
User Security Question Status Report Form
Entitlement Owner Access Review Report Form

entry.value: script: return signoffRequired == null || signoffRequired == false

Field.name: approvalSchemeField
Field.Description: This field is dynamic based on the type of workflow, as the approval scheme values is the only real difference between what we need for Create and Update vs. normal provisioning. The type is injected into the rule/script context by the WorkflowBean.

Field.name: approvalScheme
Field.Description: This is the value that gets set on the initalizer based on the approvalSchemeField. Since we store a csv we do a csv conversion of the values in approvalScheme field.

Field.name: approverElectronicSignature
Field.Description: This field is only interesting from LCMIdentity and LCMRegistration workflows, hidden otherwise.

Field.name: notificationScheme
Field.Description: This is the value that gets set on the initalizer based on the notificationSchemeField. Since we store a csv we do a csv conversion of the values in approvalScheme field.

entry.value: script: return sailpoint.tools.Util.nullSafeEq( policiesToCheckField, "checkAll") || sailpoint.tools.Util.nullSafeEq( policyScheme, "none");

Field.name: notificationScheme
Field.Description: This is the value that gets set on the initalizer based on the notificationSchemeField. Since we store a csv we do a csv conversion of the values in approvalScheme field.

Field.name: approvalSchemeField
Field.Description: This field is dynamic based on the type of workflow, as the approval scheme values is the only real difference between what we need for Create and Update vs. normal provisioning. The type is injected into the rule/script context by the WorkflowBean.

Field.name: approvalScheme
Field.Description: This is the value that gets set on the initalizer based on the approvalSchemeField. Since we store a csv we do a csv conversion of the values in approvalScheme field.

Field.name: notificationScheme
Field.Description: This is the value that gets set on the initalizer based on the notificationSchemeField. Since we store a csv we do a csv conversion of the values in approvalScheme field.

Form.name: Edit Identity Requests Status Custom Fields
Self-service Registration Form
Form.Description: This form is used to for self-service registration.

Form.name: Group_Update_Form
Form.Description: This form is used to update groups.

Field.helpKey: Describe what access or resource this entitlement grants.
Field.name: entitlementDescription

Field.helpKey: An Entitlement can have different Governance Level. Governance Level defines what is the rigor of approval required for an Entitlement based on the classification of the assets or resources it is granting access.
Field.name: governanceLevel

List.String: GL03 - Manager and Entitlement Owner approval required
GL04 - Entitlement Owner and App Owner approval required

Field.helpKey: The person who owns the Entitlement. Owner is defined in IdentityIQ system for each Entitlement. It can be an actual Owner of the resource (application or data) or a person the resource Owner delegated the responsibility. Owner is responsible for periodic certification of the Entitlement
Field.name: entitlementOwner

Field.displayName: You want to select deputy approver/alternate approver
Field.name: selectDecision

AllowedValues.String: I do not want deputy/alternate approver

Form.name: Orbia_Group_Creation
Form.Description: This form is used to create groups.

Field.helpKey: An Entitlement can have different Governance Level. Governance Level defines what is the rigor of approval required for an Entitlement based on the classification of the assets or resources it is granting access.
Field.name: glevel

List.String: GL03 - Manager and Entitlement Owner approval required
GL04 - Entitlement Owner and App Owner approval required

Field.helpKey: The person who owns the Entitlement. Owner is defined in IdentityIQ system for each Entitlement. It can be an actual Owner of the resource (application or data) or a person the resource Owner delegated the responsibility. Owner is responsible for periodic certification of the Entitlement
Field.name: aname
